signature_chain.treatment: DuotonePhoto in charcoal+saffron per concept-architect's signature_visual
signature_chain.notes: All 5 chapter images sourced and validated. Builder should pass each through DuotonePhoto with the brief palette (charcoal #1A1A1A bg + saffron #E89B2C accent — adjust per concept-architect's final palette law).

services_image_optional_slots.note: Brief uses services-process (numbered steps). Per-service images are OPTIONAL for that variant — the layout works with icons + numerals alone. The slots below are NOT in slots_expected; if the builder decides to render images for them, asset-gatherer should be re-spawned to source them. Currently flagged 'no asset' so the builder can iconify the steps.

slots.reason: Niche (leaf-spring repair). Stock has weak coverage. Builder should iconify rather than ship a stock-mismatch.
Welding stock is plentiful but visually distinct from the truck-workshop register — sparks/MIG-arc shots break visual cohesion. Builder should iconify.
AC service stock is car-focused (R134A canisters) — wrong vehicle class. Builder should iconify.
Hydraulic-cylinder stock is generic and metal-only — adds no narrative. Builder should iconify.

images.license: owned by Conte srl (client's own brand graphic)
images.alt_text: Logo Conte srl con illustrazione a matita di un veicolo industriale e claim 'Riparazione veicoli industriali'
images.notes: This is the client's actual brand graphic — pencil-sketch heavy truck illustration with the 'Conte SRL' wordmark and 'Riparazione veicoli industriali' tagline. Used as the primary logo for the build. Raster only at 719x453, will pixelate above ~720px display width and will not retina-scale. FLAGGED needs_denis: ask client to supply original SVG / vector / hi-res of this exact mark, OR engage a designer to vector-trace.

images.attribution: Photo by cottonbro studio via Pexels
images.license: Pexels free use (commercial allowed, attribution optional)
images.alt_text: Camion rosso visto frontalmente da angolazione bassa all'interno di un'officina industriale in penombra, fari accesi, ponti sollevatori e attrezzature visibili ai lati
images.notes: Hero pick. Dark editorial industrial: dramatic low angle, strong chiaroscuro, the truck dominates the frame with workshop bays receding either side. Matches brief direction (German-engineering precision feel, NOT cheery construction-site). Truck is American-spec Peterbilt-class, but at this framing the cab silhouette reads universally 'serious heavy vehicle' — no logos visible; the brand is the workshop, not the truck. This image survives crop both wide (16:9) and tall (3:4 mobile) without losing the hero subject.

images.attribution: Photo by cottonbro studio via Pexels
images.alt_text: Meccanico in camicia a quadri solleva il cofano basculante di un camion europeo giallo per accedere al motore, vano motore e radiatore visibili
images.notes: Yellow European cab-over truck (MAN/Iveco-class) with cabin tilted forward, mechanic lifting the front grille / hood. This is *exactly* the kind of work Conte does — confirmed by Conte's own slideshow_7.png (Mercedes Atego with cab tilted). Same photographer and lighting key as the hero, so visual cohesion is preserved across hero → about.

images.alt_text: Parete di pezzi di ricambio meccanici sospesi in un'officina autentica, calendario sulla sinistra, taniche di olio Castrol Magnatec in basso, illuminazione calda da lampada appesa
images.notes: Optional atmospheric backdrop / about-secondary image / parallax filler. Cluttered authentic workshop wall covered in hanging used parts, an Italian-style wall calendar, Castrol Magnatec oil canisters at the bottom. Reads 'real working garage' rather than staged. Decorative use only — do NOT use as a primary service-card image. Builder may use as a section divider, footer band, or DuotonePhoto under a quote pull.

images.attribution: Photo by cottonbro studio via Pexels
images.alt_text: Doppia ruota di un camion vista da vicino con suspension air-bag e attrezzatura blu del banco prova in primo piano
images.notes: Heavy-truck dual rear tires close-up with air-bag suspension and a blue jack/cradle — reads as 'truck on the test bench'. Matches Conte's own slideshow_10.png (truck on actual banco prova freni).

images.attribution: Photo by Gustavo Fring via Pexels
images.alt_text: Meccanico crouched accanto alla ruota di un camion blu mentre opera uno strumento meccanico di controllo dell'allineamento, cabinet diagnostico rosso al lato
images.notes: Wheel alignment / steering geometry test on a heavy truck wheel with a red diagnostic cabinet — direct visual analog to the banco prova giochi (verifica organi di sterzo e sospensione).

images.alt_text: Tubo di scarico di un veicolo con fumi visibili in uscita, primo piano sul terminale e sulla nuvola di gas
images.notes: Exhaust tailpipe with visible smoke plume. Direct visual metaphor for the opacimetro (exhaust opacity meter — measures particulate density in exhaust). Image is a passenger car, not a truck — minor mismatch with Conte's heavy-vehicle focus, but the *concept* of 'fumi di scarico misurati' is unambiguous. Acceptable. If concept-architect or builder finds it too passenger-car, fall back to a Higgsfield generation of 'industrial truck exhaust pipe with visible smoke, dark workshop interior'.

images.attribution: Photo by cottonbro studio via Pexels
images.alt_text: Meccanico in felpa verde scuro che opera un'attrezzatura diagnostica accanto al cofano di un camion rosso, illuminazione cinematografica scura
images.notes: Mechanic in dark green hoodie operating a diagnostic device beside the red truck hero (same hero series, same red truck — visual continuity). Reads as 'professional diagnostic operation' which is what TEXA (the brand of OBD/EOBD diagnostic tester Conte uses) work looks like. Tool brand is generic in the photo so it doesn't conflict with TEXA's actual visual identity — perfect ambiguity for the slot.

images.attribution: Photo by cottonbro studio via Pexels
images.alt_text: Mani di un meccanico con chiave a brugola che lavorano su una testa motore diesel pesante in primo piano, illuminazione scura editoriale
images.notes: Hands working a wrench on a heavy diesel valve-train / cylinder head. Editorial dark close-up, deep blacks, bare hands on oil-streaked metal — peak 'mechanics' hands on engines' from the brief. Slot-mapped to revisione cambi (gearbox), but the imagery is more cylinder-head / valve-train. The component complexity reads 'gearbox internals' close enough at thumbnail/card scale; if the concept-architect would rather, swap with service-revisione-motori (similar visual register, similar story).

images.license: Unsplash license (free for commercial use, attribution appreciated)
images.alt_text: Meccanico tiene tra le mani un componente in ghisa pesante (corpo differenziale o riduttore) sopra un banco di lavoro con superficie blu
images.notes: Mechanic's hands holding a cast-iron differential / reduction gear housing on an industrial workbench. Reads literally as 'differential repair'. Cast-iron texture and blue worktable echo the magazzino / workshop palette.

images.alt_text: Mano con guanto arancione di un meccanico sul mozzo di una ruota di un veicolo, dettaglio del disco freno e mozzo metallico
images.notes: Gloved-hand close-up on a vehicle wheel hub / brake-rotor mounting surface. Reads 'brake system service' clearly. Subject is a passenger car / SUV not a heavy truck — minor mismatch with Conte's truck focus, but the brake-component close-up is universal. Hand-on-component framing matches the visual rhythm of revisione-cambi and revisione-differenziali (also hand-on-component shots) → gives the three revisione cards a coherent triptych.

images.attribution: Photo by Artem Podrez via Pexels
images.alt_text: Mani di un meccanico in tuta blu sopra una testa motore di un quattro cilindri con bilancieri visibili, primo piano dell'aluminio lavorato
images.notes: Hands above a 4-cylinder cylinder head, clean machined aluminum, valve seats visible — straight 'engine overhaul' read. Pairs with the dark-handsy revisione-cambi for the engine/transmission service block.

images.attribution: Photo by cottonbro studio via Pexels
images.alt_text: Meccanico in felpa verde scuro alza il cofano di un camion rosso in un'officina con foschia atmosferica, scena cinematografica con fari accesi
images.notes: Same red truck + green-hoodie mechanic series, this frame more cinematic with smoke / haze in foreground and the mechanic mid-action lifting the hood. Mapped to 'interventi esterni' (on-site assistance / mobile service) loosely — the implied narrative of 'mechanic arriving / going to work the vehicle' supports a mobile-service framing. If the builder finds the slot a poor fit, swap with the about slot or use this as a secondary hero / banner.

images.attribution: Photo by Gustavo Fring via Pexels
images.alt_text: Meccanico in camicia a quadri seduto nella cabina di un camion blu mentre consulta una checklist su tablet, vista laterale dal lato cabina con lo specchio che riflette l'officina
images.notes: Mechanic in plaid + blue truck cab consulting a tablet/checklist — reads as 'professional in-cab inspection / verification', which is what a tachigrafo digitale calibration check looks like procedurally. Plaid + blue cab match the cottonbro studio palette of the rest of the truck-workshop set, so visual cohesion holds. The tachigrafo device itself is not visible — the moment is the procedural one. CRITICAL slot: this fills chapter 04 of the signature scroll-chain ('Tachigrafi digitali I3 044 1168'). If concept-architect wants the actual tachigrafo device shown, regenerate via Higgsfield: prompt 'close-up of digital tachograph DTCO display unit installed in heavy truck dashboard, Italian text labels, dim cab interior, dark editorial photo, no people no other text', model nano_banana_2, ~3 cr.

images.attribution: Photo by Artem Podrez via Pexels
images.alt_text: Mani di un meccanico in tuta blu sopra una testa motore con bilancieri visibili, primo piano dell'aluminio lavorato — revisione meccanica integrale
images.notes: ALIAS of service-revisione-motori. The brief (researcher's brief.json) consolidated 'Revisione motori, cambi e differenziali' into a single service entry called 'Revisione meccanica integrale' (chapter 03 of the signature scroll-chain). Same image file content as service-revisione-motori — duplicated under this name so the builder can call it directly. The granular cambi/differenziali images remain in the manifest as backup for builder choice.

images.alt_text: Magazzino di cinghie trapezoidali e di trasmissione impilate in dense pile su scaffalature in legno, illuminazione calda smorzata
images.notes: Dense V-belt storage shelving — labeled coils stacked floor-to-ceiling. Mirrors Conte's own slideshow_5.png magazzino interior (real reference). Cluttered, atmospheric, *real* — does NOT read 'IKEA-clean Amazon warehouse' which would have been wrong for a small-town heavy-truck workshop in Puglia.

flags_for_denis: logo: client-website source is a 719x453 raster pencil-sketch — pixelates above ~720px and will not retina-scale. Action: ask client (or commission a designer) for an SVG / vector trace of the existing 'Conte SRL' wordmark + truck illustration. Until then, the build will display the raster at small sizes (header ≤320px wide, footer, og:image source) only. If the concept-architect picks a hero treatment that demands a large logo (e.g. monolith on the hero), flag back so we can reprioritise.

service-opacimetro: chosen image (Pexels 5233283) shows a passenger-car exhaust pipe with smoke. Concept is correct (fumi di scarico misurati) but the vehicle is not heavy. If concept-architect deems it off-brand, generate a Higgsfield replacement: prompt 'industrial truck tailpipe close-up, visible diesel smoke plume, dark workshop interior, Sebastião Salgado documentary style, no people no text', model nano_banana_2 or cinematic_studio_2_5, ~2-3 cr.

service-revisione-impianti-frenanti: chosen image is a passenger-car wheel hub close-up; the gloved-hand framing is universal but vehicle is not heavy. Acceptable as-is for editorial coherence with the other 'hands-on-component' service triptych. If unacceptable, generate a Higgsfield replacement of a heavy-truck disc brake hub.

Stock tier 1 (Unsplash) and tier 2 (Pexels) yielded 13/14 images at 'high' quality — Higgsfield not needed for this build. Cottonbro studio's truck-workshop series (multiple photos: 7019376, 7019372, 7019610, 7541350, 7541343, 7019604, 7019364, 7564871) gave us six visually-cohesive frames in the same dark-editorial workshop register. This kept credit spend at zero.

Source data has NO team and NO testimonials — analysis.json team[] and testimonials[] are both empty. Asset-gatherer correctly did NOT generate stock 'team' or 'avatar' images. The brief / concept-architect should DROP team and testimonials sections entirely; do not stub with stock people. (Same lesson as Krefer 2026-05-03 procedural memory.)

Industry confidence in analysis is 0.55 (analyzer flagged ambiguous between 'construction' and 'automotive workshop'). The asset selection committed to the heavy-vehicle/automotive-workshop reading. If the master agent and Denis decide on a different starter (e.g. professional-services), several service slots (banco prova freni, opacimetro, TEXA) lose their literal anchor and the concept-architect will need to re-scope which slots survive.

Brief.json finalised AFTER initial manifest. Researcher consolidated some services and added new ones (Lavorazione balestre, Saldature, Climatizzazione, Trasmissione idraulica, Tachigrafi digitali). Asset-gatherer added 'service-tachigrafi' (CRITICAL — chapter 04 of the signature scroll-chain) and an alias 'service-revisione-meccanica' for the consolidated motori+cambi+differenziali entry. The four NEW services (balestre, saldature, climatizzazione, trasmissione-idraulica) are listed under 'services_image_optional_slots' but NO image was sourced — the brief's services-process variant is image-optional and the builder should iconify those four numbered steps. If the builder ships images for them anyway, re-spawn asset-gatherer with explicit slot requests.

Signature visual is a HorizontalScrollTrack of 5 chapters (TEXA → banco prova freni+opacimetro → revisione meccanica → tachigrafi → interventi esterni). All 5 chapter image slots are filled and recorded in the 'signature_chain' block of this manifest. Builder should pass each through DuotonePhoto with the brief palette (charcoal + saffron). All 5 photos are from cottonbro studio's truck-workshop series OR consistent low-key tonality — the duotone treatment will further unify them.

Hero variant per brief is hero-stats (lead with verifiable numbers: 25+ anni, codice tecnico I3 044 1168, 3 telefoni operativi). The hero image (red truck low-angle) backs that block as a DuotonePhoto background or split-side photo — both work.
attributions_required: Photo by cottonbro studio via Pexels — used for: hero, about, service-banco-prova-freni, service-tester-diagnostico-texa, service-revisione-cambi, service-interventi-esterni
Photo by Gustavo Fring via Pexels — used for: service-banco-prova-giochi
Photo by Artem Podrez via Pexels — used for: service-revisione-motori
Photos via Pexels (uncredited contributors) — used for: atmosphere, service-opacimetro, service-magazzino-ricambi
Photos via Unsplash — used for: service-revisione-differenziali, service-revisione-impianti-frenanti
_notes: Brief.json was not yet written when this manifest was produced — researcher and asset-gatherer ran in parallel per pipeline spec. The slot inventory was derived from analysis.json (services list of 10 + about + atmosphere + hero + logo). If the researcher's brief.json reduces or renames service slots, the builder should reconcile by either (a) renaming files to match brief slot ids, or (b) leaving unmapped service images in /images/ unused without re-fetching.

Visual cohesion: 7 of 14 images come from the cottonbro studio workshop series (same red and yellow trucks, same dark editorial lighting key, same colour temperature). The remaining picks (Artem Podrez engine, Gustavo Fring alignment, Unsplash differential & brake-hub) were filtered to similar low-key tonality. Across the 14 images the dominant palette is: deep charcoal/black, oxidised red/orange (truck bodywork), worn industrial blue (workshop equipment), warm sodium-vapor light. This matches the 'dark editorial industrial' direction in the brief.

No videos or lotties were sourced — analysis suggests a still-image hero is sufficient. If the concept-architect chooses HeroVideo as a component, asset-gatherer should be re-spawned to source a Pexels video loop ('truck workshop interior moving lights' or 'mechanic working dark interior', 4-15s loop, ≥1920px).
